findings: [GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/owner' members=['allUsers']
Action wildcard '*' found — grants all actions on all services
Resource wildcard '*' found — applies to all AWS resources
CRITICAL: Action '*' with Resource '*' — full unrestricted access
Allow statement has no Condition block — consider adding MFA or IP restrictions
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Principal '*' — allows any entity including anonymous internet users to assume this role
Potential for lateral movement and privilege escalation due to overly permissive policy
Lack of audit and logging capabilities to monitor and detect unauthorized access
[GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/editor' members=['allAuthenticatedUsers']
Action wildcard '*' found — grants all actions on all services
Resource wildcard '*' found — applies to all AWS resources
CRITICAL: Action '*' with Resource '*' — full unrestricted access
Allow statement has no Condition block — consider adding MFA or IP restrictions
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Principal '*' — allows any entity including anonymous internet users to assume this role
Potential for lateral movement and privilege escalation due to overly permissive policy
Lack of audit and logging capabilities to monitor and detect unauthorized access
[GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/storage.admin' members=['allUsers']
Resource wildcard '*' found — applies to all AWS resources
Allow statement has no Condition block — consider adding MFA or IP restrictions
Service wildcard 's3:*' — grants all actions for this service
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Principal '*' — allows any entity including anonymous internet users to assume this role
Potential for data exfiltration due to unrestricted S3 access
Lack of resource-specific permissions, which could lead to unintended access
[GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/storage.objectViewer' members=['user:alice@example.com'] condition='resource.name.startsWith('projects/my-project')'
Broad S3 resource 'arn:aws:s3:::*' — applies to all buckets
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Unrestricted principal with wildcard (*) in the AWS account ID
Unused or irrelevant condition '_gcp_condition' from a different cloud provider (GCP)

results.description: Pub/Sub subscriber for specific service account
findings: [GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/pubsub.subscriber' members=['serviceAccount:my-app@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com']
Allow statement has no Condition block — consider adding MFA or IP restrictions
Broad resource pattern 'arn:aws:sqs:*:*:*' — consider restricting to specific resources
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Principal is based on a wildcard (*) which could lead to unintended access
No expiration or rotation policy for the role

results.description: IAM serviceAccountUser on allAuthenticatedUsers with condition
findings: [GCP Policy] Translated from GCP IAM bindings format
[GCP Binding] role='roles/iam.serviceAccountUser' members=['allAuthenticatedUsers'] condition='request.time < timestamp('2025-12-31T00:00:00Z')'
Broad IAM resource 'arn:aws:iam::*:*' — applies to all IAM resources in account
No explicit Deny statements — consider adding Deny rules for sensitive operations
Principal '*' — allows any entity including anonymous internet users to assume this role
The policy allows assume role from any entity in us-east-1 region without MFA
The _gcp_condition is not a valid AWS condition and may cause unexpected behavior
The policy does not specify a valid AWS condition for the request time, as 'request.time' is not a valid key
